rawIndexableTextContent: A minimal stack for Core Data. Overview This package has these classes:  They exist to simplify writing a CRUD with Core Data. Usage Writing a managed model Let’s say your API produces a Codable model that you want to persist. Start by writing an equivalent managed object model. Given a Codable model:  Write equivalent objects as NSManagedObject subclasses:  Then create a Model.xcdatamodeld file using Xcode Core Data editor. Initializing the container Use the Model.xcdatamodeld you created before to initialize the PersistentContainer:  Sidenote: if your model is in a target and you expect to test it from a test target, you’ll have to expose the Bundle.module of the target. This is unrelated to Core Data or this library. The test targets have their own bundle and can’t access the bundles of other targets. Adding a class like this inside the target should be enough:  Mapping models and managed objects Add a convenience constructor to build the managed object with a model. This little saving will become a lot once you write many objects.  From here, mapping a model to a managed object is trivial:  Writing models to Core Data At this point we can persist objects like this:  Or, given that our model implements Persistable, let the container do the mapping for you:  Reading models from Core Data To read the entities call read(). The type will be inferred.
title: CoreDataStack
summary: A minimal stack for Core Data.

rawIndexableTextContent: Return entities matching a predicate.  Return Value Zero or more managed objects. The predicate for a fetch request. Discussion The queried entity is passed as a generic parameter.
title: read(predicate:)

rawIndexableTextContent: Register the transformers in this file.
title: register()
summary: Register the transformers in this file.

rawIndexableTextContent: Managed object where this model will be stored.
title: MO
summary: Managed object where this model will be stored.

rawIndexableTextContent: Maps this instance to a managed object ‘MO’ that has the same properties and can be persisted.  Return Value A managed object with the same properties, or nil if this model is invalid. A managed context where this object is fetched or created. Discussion Note that this method has the following responsabilities: Return nil if the model doesn’t satisfy the constraints of the managed object. Upsert if needed. For instance, if the MO defines a unique property constraint you should fetch and update instead create a new one. Set (or update) the MO properties with those of the model.
title: mapPersistable(context:)
summary: Maps this instance to a managed object ‘MO’ that has the same properties and can be persisted.

rawIndexableTextContent: Defines model objects that may be persisted.
title: Persistable
summary: Defines model objects that may be persisted.

rawIndexableTextContent: Contains a reversible transformer between NSNumber and NSString.
title: CoreDataTransformers
summary: Contains a reversible transformer between NSNumber and NSString.

rawIndexableTextContent: Creates a persistence container.  Name of the .xcdatamodeld directory containg your model, without the .xcdatamodeld extension. True to create a store in memory that won’t be persisted Bundle containing your model. Discussion A note about testing: if the model is inside a package, in order to test it you will have to expose the Bundle.module with a reference from a class inside that package. e.g.  See https://stackoverflow.com/questions/58258965/71298555#71298555 for details.
title: init(name:inMemory:bundle:)

rawIndexableTextContent: Loads the persistent stores.  Discussion See PersistentContainer.loadPersistentStores(). Parameters retry: true to attempt recovery by deleting the previous store. completionHandler: Once the loading of the persistent stores has completed, this block will be executed on the calling thread.
title: loadPersistentStores(retry:completionHandler:)

rawIndexableTextContent: Return Value true if this is a migration error.
title: isMigrationError

rawIndexableTextContent: Save the given array of Persistable instances.  objects to be mapped and saved as managed objects.
title: save(model:)
summary: Save the given array of Persistable instances.

rawIndexableTextContent: The only error thrown by this persistence stack.  Overview By wrapping all errors of a layer in a single error, you can recognize the origin of any catched error.
title: PersistenceError
summary: The only error thrown by this persistence stack.

rawIndexableTextContent: Return all entities of the given generic type.  Return Value Zero or more managed objects. Discussion The queried entity is passed as a generic parameter.
title: read()
summary: Return all entities of the given generic type.
